        <b-checkbox v-model="hasError">Show errors</b-checkbox>

        <b-field
            label="Username"
            :type="{ 'is-danger': hasError }"
            :message="{ 'Username is not available': hasError }"
        >
            <b-input model-value="johnsilver" maxlength="30"></b-input>
        </b-field>

        <b-field
            label="Password"
            :type="{ 'is-danger': hasError }"
            :message="[
                { 'Password is too short': hasError },
                { 'Password must have at least 8 characters': hasError },
            ]"
        >
            <b-input model-value="123" type="password" maxlength="30"></b-input>
        </b-field>
    </section>

ion>
        <b-field>
            <b-switch v-model="isAmPm">AM/PM</b-switch>
        </b-field>
        <b-field label="Select time">
            <b-clockpicker
                v-model="time"
                placeholder="Click to select..."
                :hour-format="format"
            >
                <b-button
                    label="Now"
                    type="is-primary"
                    icon-left="clock"
                    @click="time = new Date()"
                />
                <b-button
                    label="Clear"
                    type="is-danger"
                    icon-left="close"
                    outlined
                    @click="time = null"
                />
            </b-clockpicker>
        </b-field>
    </section>
</template

Items can be created / modified / deleted and will always keep the defined order.

You can also use v-if to show (or not) a Step Item. In this case, you have to specify order to maintain the consistent order.
